mnemonics: DACs Decide: Decentralized Arbitration, Centralized Selection
keyPoints: Bus arbitration resolves conflicts when multiple devices request bus control simultaneously.
Centralized arbitration uses a single bus controller (arbiter) to grant access.
Decentralized arbitration allows devices to determine priority among themselves using a daisy-chain or parallel scheme.
commonMistakes: Assuming the CPU is always the bus master; other devices like DMA controllers can also be masters.
Confusing bus arbitration (who gets control) with bus multiplexing (how data is routed).
Thinking that a higher-priority device in a daisy-chain can be 'starved'; its request will always propagate to the arbiter.
